insight title: New STOM Bureau
insight description: Dr Mehdi Balti as new president of STOM explained to me that the new bureau is setting up the priorities and is open to work with Roche for new partnerships. I shared with him the proposals and proposed to meet again to align on the action plan.
hco: Hôpital Militaire de Tunis - Oncology Dept

insight title: New national strategy for gynecology services
insight description: New national strategy for big gynecology services to choose sub-specialties and work in « poles ». This means that each center will specialise in a given DA. For example in Sousse they chose onco-gynecology and reproduction.
hco: Hôpital Farhat Hached - Obstetrics & Gynaecology Dept

insight title: Increasing trend in patients with bening vs malign breast tumors
insight description: According to Dr Bouguizane the number of patients with malign breast cancer treated in his service is increasing compared to benign ones. He estimates that the split is around 70% malign vs 30% benign.
hco: Hôpital Farhat Hached - Obstetrics & Gynaecology Dept

insight title: Monthly educational session for the gynecologists
insight description: The gynecology service plans a monthly training session for the residents and interns. Each month a different topic is chosen to be discussed. In May a breast cancer is planned and Roche was invited to attend.
hco: Hôpital Farhat Hached - Obstetrics & Gynaecology Dept

insight title: Big Onco-gynecology Educational days are planned for October 2025
insight description: Dr Bouguizane is planning a two days educational event with Dr Badis Channoufi and other gynecologists. The attendance is estimated at 550 between medical students and HCPs. The event will take place on 24-25 october and a big focus will be given to oncology. Roche is invited to participate and invite a speaker to present the management of early BC and the latest recommendations.
hco: Hôpital Farhat Hached - Obstetrics & Gynaecology Dept

insight title: Kadcyla use in metastatic patients
insight description: Currently Kadcyla is indictaed to patients in the adjuvent setting in Sousse and not to patients in the metastatic indictation. According to Dr Ben Fatma this is due mainly to lack of knowledge and training. A staff meeting will be organised by Roche in the concerned center to address this matter.

insight title: Radiology/mammography in Jendouba
insight description: New radiology agreement in Jendouba to unlock the lack of specialists - During a meeting with STOM bureau, which Dr Najet Mahjoub belongs to as treasury secretary, she mentioned that they have signed a new agreement with the radiology service of Ben Arous hospital in order to help them with the interpretation of mammographies through teleradiology (remote radiology). The local technicians will manipulate the patients to perform the mammographies and the radiologists in Ben Arous will interpret them and provide their findings.
hco: Hopital regional jandouba

insight title: Roche_STOM meeting
insight description:
During our official meeting with the new bureau of STOM we discussed their priorities for the upcoming 2 years - The priorities:
- launch yearly educational days specific to each oncology disease. This year they will start with BC.
- paramedics education 
- organize an international oncology event in Tunisia in April 2026 
- co-create with roche the BC awareness campaign 
- sit with CNAM and discuss the pending reimbursments dossiers to understand the delays
hco: Hôpital Militaire de Tunis - Oncology Dept

insight title: Kadcyla use_Kairouan experience

hco: Cabinet Dr Rim Chafai

insight title: ePOSTER on HER2+ accepted in ESMO BC
insight description:
Dr Wiem prepared an ePOSTER that was accepted by ESMO BC, below the summary of the results:
Over a 12-year period (2012–2023), 107 patients with HER2-overexpressing breast cancer were included, with a mean age of 51 years. Twenty-three patients (21.5%) presented with metastatic disease at diagnosis. Invasive ductal carcinoma accounted for 91.6% of cases. Curative surgery was performed in 98,9% of patients, combined with neoadjuvant chemotherapy (52.5%), adjuvant chemotherapy (95,7%), hormone therapy (47,8%), and radiotherapy (85%). Trastuzumab was administered in the neoadjuvant setting in 44 patients and in the adjuvant setting in 97 patients, whereas pertuzumab was used in only 2 cases. - The mean overall survival was 140 months significantly better in postmenopausal patients (p = 0,02) and in those with metastatic disease (p = 0,006). Disease-free survival reached 191 months and was significantly better in patients who achieved a pathological complete response (p = 0,023). Among metastatic patients, SBR grade (p = 0,002), timing of metastasis (p = 0,007), and the use of adjuvant trastuzumab (p = 0,017) significantly influenced survival. In multivariate analysis, menopausal status emerged as an independent factor associated with improved overall survival (p = 0,011).
hco: Hôpital Universitaire Fattouma Bourguiba de Monastir - Oncology Dept

insight title: Endophthalmitis risk with intravitreal injection
insight description:
Even with strict precautionary measures, the risk of endophthalmitis is always present after an intravitreal injection.
It's broadly categorized into two types:
 * Infectious Endophthalmitis: This is the more serious type and is caused by the introduction of microorganisms (bacteria, fungi) into the eye during the injection.
 * Inflammatory Endophthalmitis: This type is characterized by inflammation within the eye but without any identifiable infection. It's often milder than infectious endophthalmitis, with less pain and a better visual prognosis, as it typically resolves spontaneously or with anti-inflammatory treatment.
Despite rigorous measures, the risk can never be completely eliminated due to various factors, including the patient's own bacterial flora, potential microscopic contamination,…

insight title: Eylea Cold Chain Integrity: Challenges with Patient Transport
insight description:
Dr Knani explains that when patients receive their Eylea boxes, they transport the treatment to the hospital. The cold chain isn't strictly maintained by patients during this process, which impacts the product's integrity. 
To ensure proper preservation, patients leave their medication at the hospital service rather than taking it home. This way, when they arrive for their injection, their treatment is already there.
Ideally, these medications should be stored at the hospital from the outset to guarantee the cold chain.
hco: Hôpital Farhat Hached - Ophthalmology Dept

insight title: scientific agenda for Retina session during STO congress
insight description:
Dr Fekih announced that she will be organizing a retina battle during STO congress "should we start with new A-VEGFs -Afli8mg & Faricimab- or not?", Bayer will be supporting the session by inviting an international expert to participate in it, We declined the offer, as she mentioned that she already moved on with the planification and experts hiring. Dr Fekih announced that she will be inviting Pr Aude Couturier to speak for Faricimab early adoption.
Regarding STO participation she mentioned that it is managed by Pr Errais only, but she will be interested in knowing more about our scientific agenda during the congress.
Regarding STO-Bayer webinar, Dr Fekih mentioned that Bayer were mostly a sponsor and did not interfere with the session, and she did herself the briefing of the experts and the managed the preparation of the event with bayer's support. finally she mentions that she remains open to scientific collaboration for future scientific events.
hco: Institut Hédi Raïs d'Ophtalmologie (IHROT)

insight title: STO congress participation

Symposia plan and owners not defined as of end of December, but will be finalized early in 2026.

He finally highlighted the strong partnership already established and the trust.
He invites us to align more and communicate the amount in the first 15 days of January.
Amounts will be the only criteria for prioritization as he wants to keep it equitable for all sponsors.

insight title: Inflammation Biomarkers identification for treatment prioritization
insight description:
Dr messaoud highlights the established and strong pipeline of Roche in Ophthalmology and is curious about the timelines for the availability of Faricimab in Tunisia.
Dr messaoud highlighted the inflammatory component of retinal disease, suggesting that Ang2 inhibition has a strong potential to alleviate patient's burden and to induce more impactful efficacy for many patients. He also highlighted the Potential for Anti-IL6 in the management of not only UME, but also DME.
Dr Messaoud says that in the future we will be able to identify biomarkers to identify the patients that will have more inflammation than others, allowing to prioritize the treatments that have more anti-inflammatory action than others, and thus to prioritize CS injections and implants. When asked about the important side effect potential of CS, he replied that cataract may occur with age anyway, and would be easily manageable and acceptable if a patient is losing his VA and may benefit from CS. - Dr Messaoud acknowledges that identifiying biomarkers to select those patients who have a better potential to benefit from anti-inflammatory treatment would be a path forward to bring more efficacy to them while mitigating the high risk of side effects.
this will be possible only through clinical trials designed to identify those biomarkers.
Category_ML: Medical Education, Access & Policy, Strategic Partnership
hco: Hôpital Universitaire Tahar Sfar

insight title: Optimizing Access to Eylea: The Role of Reference Pharmacies in the Dispensing
insight description: When a physician prescribes Eylea, the patient visits a retail pharmacy. However, due to the high cost of the product, not all pharmacies keep it in stock. Only a few maintain a permanent inventory, enabling immediate or 24-hour dispensing. Consequently, ophthalmologists naturally direct their patients toward these key reference pharmacies.
hco: Cabinet Privé d'Ophtalmologie de Dr Afef Bouziri

RVO (OVCR): 10%
hco: Cabinet Privé d'Ophtalmologie de Dr Afef Bouziri

insight title: IVT Patient Access: Transitioning IVT Dispensing to Retail Pharmacies
insight description: A few years ago, ophthalmic clinics maintained an internal inventory of IVT drugs to ensure immediate patient access. However, as these products were handled as non-returnable firm orders (commande ferme), the Ministry of Health subsequently prohibited this practice. Consequently, patients must now take their prescriptions to a retail pharmacy (near to the clinic) to obtain their treatment—usually within 24 hours—before returning to the clinic for the injection

insight title: The Hybrid Care Model: Analyzing Algerian and Libyan Patient Flows in Tunis
insight description:
At the Tunis Ophthalmology Clinic, we observe two distinct profiles of foreign patients (Algerian and Libyan)
The patient's choice between these two options is usually a balance between technical trust and logistical constraints :
1. Choice of Immediate Injection in Tunis
Many patients prefer to have the first injection performed on-site immediately, provided the medication is available.
• Cold Chain Guarantee
• Technical Mastery
2. Choice of Diagnosis Only (Follow-up in Home Country)
In this scenario, the patient receives a full workup in Tunis but seeks treatment locally.
• Recurring Costs: Retinal pathologies require repeated injections—often monthly. Making the trip every time becomes financially and physically unsustainable.
• Increased Local Availability: Many patients visit Tunis specifically for "Diagnostic Expertise" to obtain a precise treatment protocol. They then have this protocol administered by their local ophthalmologist
hco: Clinique ophthalmologique de Tunis

insight title: Local & regional challenges in the management of HA
insight description:
-the objective of the care provided to PwHA in Tunisia is driven by equity (access to care for all, beyond status, severity, or geography). Pr Gouider highlighted pricing efforts are required from Roche. 
-partnership agreement in place WFH-MOH centered around: the access to innovative treatments and diagnostic tools, Raising awareness , and the education for healthcare professionals and patients.
-Gaps on the patient journey remain unaddressed in Tunisia & the region:
Reducing the age of diagnosis, social taboos, educating on bleeding signs recognition, familial investigations.
hco: hôpital Aziza othmana

insight title: Patient with Von Willebrand disease type 3 will be receiving Hemlibra
insight description:
We have a new patient who has just received approval to begin Hemlibra treatment for Von Willebrand Disease (VWD) type 3.
This was a critical, off-label decision driven entirely by the HCP’s clinical initiative and collaboration with her peers. The patient has been suffering from frequent, severe, and life-threatening bleeding episodes, making this access a potential life-saving turning point.
Indeed, this is not an indication that we promote.
hco: Hôpital Farhat Hached - Haematology Dept
